title: 32 Hours To Create, 10 Minutes To Destroy: Deepfake Evidence
description:
DOCUMENTED: The Rise of Deepfake Crimes.

Episode 62 of The Evidence Room: Digital Evidence.

Every case. Every document. Every piece of evidence — animated.

0:00 Case File Opened
1:30 The Subject
4:12 First Anomalies
6:45 The Evidence Builds
9:00 Key Document Revealed
11:42 The Full Record
13:30 Verdict

Subscribe to The Evidence Room.

🌑 Dark psychological horror: youtube.com/@BetrayalDeepDive
🧠 Psychology documentaries: youtube.com/@TheControlFiles

📺 New investigation every weekday.

— LINKS —
▸ BetterHelp therapy: https://betterhelp.com/deepdive
▸ NordVPN privacy: https://nordvpn.com/deepdive
▸ CuriosityStream docs: https://curiositystream.com/deepdive
▸ Audible audiobooks: https://amzn.to/deepdive-audible

*Affiliate links — support the channel at no cost to you.

⚠️ AI-assisted narration and forensic analysis.
script_clean: Case number 2018-001 filed on January 27, 2018. Cybercrime Unit in Los Angeles received it. The discovery was made in a small office. Forensic experts tracked unusual transactions. What led to the exposé of a deepfake crime ring? It affected 543 confirmed victims over 2191 days. The crime ring evaded detection for 5 years. The entity is a social media platform launched in 2012. It connected people across the globe. The platform had routine updates and new feature releases. Users shared thoughts and photos with one another. Algorithms and moderators watched over users. But a subtle wrongness began to creep in. Only a few people noticed it. Even fewer reported it. Small discrepancies appeared. Each one was explainable. Collectively they painted a picture. A user reported a strange message. It was dismissed as a glitch. Another user claimed to have seen a fake video. It was taken down before verification. A third user noticed account access. The security team assured it was a minor breach. Anomalies continued to pile up. Moderators noticed a pattern. Management ignored warnings. The 32-page incident report dated August 2015 emerged. Documents arrived and records were pulled. Each one revealed deceit and corruption. Forty-seven reports of suspicious activity were filed. Only three were investigated. None led to significant action. The case file revealed a shocking truth. Management had been aware of the deepfake problem. They chose to ignore it. Profits were prioritized over people. Evidence Builds The evidence started building on February 10, 2015. A pattern of suspicious activity emerged. Twenty-seven reports were filed in 2015. Only two were investigated. In 2016, twenty reports were filed. Only one was investigated. In 2017, thirty-seven reports were filed. None were investigated. Investigator John Smith reacted with surprise. He said "the pattern is clear". The platform's management was aware. They chose to do nothing. On March 15, 2018, investigator Jane Doe found a key document. It was a memo from the platform's CEO. The memo revealed a plan to ignore deepfakes. The plan was to prioritize profits. Investigator Jane Doe reacted with anger. She said "this is a clear case of negligence". The evidence continued to build. It became clear that the platform failed. The failure resulted in 1.1 million in losses. The losses occurred over seven years. The evidence revealed 543 confirmed victims. Many more were likely affected. On April 2, 2018, investigators found 147 fake accounts. They were created between 2015 and 2017. The fake accounts were used for scams. The scams resulted in 250,000 in losses. Investigator John Smith said "this is just the tip". He said "there is more to uncover". The investigation continued for 12 months. It involved 10 investigators and 5 experts. They analyzed 100,000 lines of code. They reviewed 500,000 user reports. The evidence revealed a complex scheme. It was very large and very bad. Human Cost The human cost was very high. Victims suffered emotionally. They lost money and trust. Some victims lost everything. The emotional toll was massive. Victims needed support and help. The platform did not provide it. They were left to suffer alone. The human cost was very sad. It was a very big problem. The platform must do better. They must protect their users. The human cost is too high. It must be reduced now. People are very angry. They demand action now. The platform must act fast. They must fix the problem. The human cost is too much. It must be stopped now. Very quickly and very soon. The platform must try harder. They must care about users. The human cost is very bad. It must be fixed now. With new rules and laws. To protect users and data. The human cost is too high. It must be reduced fast. The platform must do more. They must work harder now. To stop the deepfakes and scams. And to protect the users. The human cost is very high. It must be fixed now. With action and support. For the victims and users. The platform must try harder. They must care about users. And protect them from harm. The human cost is too much. It must be stopped now. Very quickly and very soon. With new rules and laws. To protect users and data. And to stop the deepfakes.
